title: hibbertia setifera
intro: Hibbertia setifera, also known as the 'needle flower' or 'tick-tongue', is a small shrub native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia setifera include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Hibbertia setifera are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the plant in a decoction to create teas, tinctures, or ointments.
